chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Liability of One Who Carried Something and Claimed Its Loss

text: 3931 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَسْتَأْجِرُ اَلْحَمَّالَ فَيَكْسِرُ اَلَّذِي يَحْمِلُ عَلَيْهِ أَوْ يُهَرِيقُهُ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ مَأْمُوناً فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَ إِنْ كَانَ غَيْرَ مَأْمُونٍ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3931 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah (as), regarding a man who hires a porter, and the porter either breaks what he is carrying or spills it.
Imam (as) said: "If he is trustworthy, then he is not liable. But if he is not trustworthy, then he is liable."
